task:
PHASE 0 (BLOCKING foundation) of the model-tier priority: add tier metadata to the adapter `listModels` contract. This atom is metadata ONLY — no resolver, no dispatch wiring, no UI. Those are later atoms; do not build them now.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. `packages/core/src/adapter/types.ts`: extend the adapter contract so an adapter can declare a tier->concrete-model mapping. RECOMMENDED shape (deviate only with a one-line justification in your summary): add an optional field to `ModelListResult`:
     readonly tiers?: Readonly<Record<string, string>>
   where the KEY is a shared tier name from a canonical vocabulary (start with `default` and `strong`) and the VALUE is THIS adapter's concrete model name for that tier (value may be the empty string to mean the CLI default). Also define and export a small core type for the canonical tier names (e.g. `export type ModelTier = 'default' | 'strong'` plus an exported `MODEL_TIERS` array) so downstream atoms and adapters share ONE vocabulary owner — do NOT let each adapter invent its own tier strings. Document the field with a comment matching the existing JSDoc style, including that omitting `tiers` means the adapter does not participate in tier resolution (opt-in).

2. `packages/adapters/src/{claude,codex,cursor-agent}.ts`: have each of these three shipped adapters populate `tiers` in its `listModels()` result with a sensible mapping to a real model that CLI supports (e.g. claude: default->'' or sonnet, strong->opus; codex: default->'', strong->its strongest; cursor-agent likewise). Use models the adapter/CLI actually accepts. `packages/adapters/src/exec.ts` is the generic exec adapter — leave it WITHOUT tiers (it legitimately does not participate); add a one-line comment noting that omission is intentional.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Change is purely ADDITIVE: `ModelListResult.models` stays `readonly string[]` and every existing consumer compiles unchanged. Concrete `{cli, model}` pins keep working byte-for-byte.
- Tier vocabulary has exactly ONE owner (the core type/array); adapters import it, they do not redeclare literals. The same tier name resolves to DIFFERENT concrete models across the three adapters (this is what a Phase-1/5 test will assert).
- No adapter base class and no shared-adapter refactor (explicitly guarded against by the priority — premature DRY at N=3). Touch only the contract type + the four adapter files.
- `pnpm -w typecheck` (or the repo's typecheck) passes, `pnpm -w test` passes, and `node scripts/proof-headless-lane.mjs` stays green. Run these and paste the exit results in your summary.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: existing adapter behavior, `listModels` enumerate semantics, preflight, build, and the headless-lane proof. In your summary, list the exact files changed and the concrete tier->model mapping you chose per adapter so I can verify the cross-CLI divergence.

PHASE 1 of the model-tier priority: assignment `tier` shape + a general, PURE tier resolver + collapse-detection primitive, with unit tests. SCOPE GUARD: do NOT wire this into live dispatch (`plays/dispatch.ts`, `runner/runner.ts`, `daemon/oz-host.ts`) and do NOT touch UI — those are Phase 2 and Phase 3. This atom only adds the shape, the pure resolver, the collapse primitive, and tests. NOTE: the old `p2-dispatch.ts`/`resolveTopTier`/`resolveDeepReadAssignments` symbols referenced in older notes have been DELETED from the tree — do not try to revive or import them; author the resolver fresh.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. `packages/core/src/personas/types.ts`: add an optional `tier?: ModelTier` field to BOTH `PersonaAssignment` and `PlayAssignment` (import `ModelTier` from the adapter contract added in Phase 0 — it is exported from `@cocoder/core`/the core index already). Document with JSDoc matching the file's style, stating the precedence rule: a non-empty concrete `model` ALWAYS wins; `tier` is consulted only when `model` is the empty string.

2. New file `packages/core/src/personas/resolve-model.ts` exporting a PURE, SYNCHRONOUS resolver and a collapse primitive (no adapter I/O inside — the caller fetches `listModels()` and passes the tier map in; this keeps it unit-testable and keeps adapter I/O at the Phase-2 seam):
   - `resolveAssignmentModel(input: { assignment: { cli: string; model: string; tier?: ModelTier }; tiers?: Readonly<Record<ModelTier, string>> }): { cli: string; model: string }` with precedence: (a) if `assignment.model` is non-empty → return `{ cli, model }` UNCHANGED (concrete pin wins, byte-for-byte as today); (b) else if `assignment.tier` is set → resolve `model = tiers[tier]` (value may be `''` meaning CLI default); if `tiers` is undefined or does not declare that tier, THROW a clear error naming the cli and tier (fail loud — never silently mis-resolve a strength request); (c) else → `{ cli, model: '' }` (CLI default).
   - A collapse-detection primitive, opt-in (callers invoke it only for adversarial pairs): e.g. `detectModelCollapse(a: { cli: string; model: string }, b: { cli: string; model: string }): boolean` returning true when the two resolve to the SAME cli AND same effective model, plus an `assertNoModelCollapse(a, b, labels: [string, string])` that throws a clear error when they collapse. Treat the empty model string as the CLI default when comparing (two empties on the same cli ARE a collapse).

3. `packages/core/src/personas/loader.ts` `resolvePlayAssignment`: carry `tier` through so the Phase-2 seam can resolve it — when a play has no override, the returned `PlayAssignment` inherits the persona assignment's `tier` (and cli+model) as today; when the play has an override, use the override's `tier`. Keep the existing concrete-inherit behavior identical when no tier is present.

4. Export `resolveAssignmentModel`, `detectModelCollapse`, `assertNoModelCollapse` from `packages/core/src/personas/index.ts` and the core index barrel (`packages/core/src/index.ts`).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (write unit tests proving each):
- Concrete-pin-wins: an assignment with non-empty `model` resolves unchanged regardless of `tier` or `tiers` — concrete pins behave exactly as today.
- Tier→concrete: with a tier set and a `tiers` map, resolves to the declared concrete model; with tier value `''` resolves to CLI default.
- Cross-CLI divergence: the SAME tier name resolves to DIFFERENT concrete models given two different adapters' tier maps (use the claude vs codex maps from Phase 0 — strong→opus vs strong→gpt-5-codex).
- Fail-loud: requesting a tier the map does not declare throws naming cli+tier.
- Collapse: `assertNoModelCollapse` throws when an adversarial pair resolves to the same {cli, model} (including two CLI-defaults on the same cli) and passes when they differ.
- `resolvePlayAssignment` propagates tier on both inherit and override paths; its no-tier behavior is unchanged.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: existing `resolvePlayAssignment`/`resolvePersona`/`resolveEffectivePersona` behavior for tier-absent assignments; no dispatch/runner/daemon/UI files touched. Run `pnpm -w typecheck` and `pnpm -w test` and paste exit results in your summary; list files changed and the resolver signature you settled on.

PHASE 2 of the model-tier priority: make tiers TAKE EFFECT at the LIVE play-dispatch seam. This atom wires play-level tier resolution only; persona-level spawn sites (runner oscar/bob, oz-host, preflight) are the NEXT atom — do NOT touch them here. Do NOT touch UI. Do NOT revive the deleted p2-dispatch path.

CONTEXT (verified live): `dispatchPlay` (`packages/core/src/plays/dispatch.ts`) is the live Play dispatcher — called from `runner.ts:1443`. Its `input.assignment` is a `PlayAssignment` which (after Phase 1) carries optional `tier`. The build site is `packages/core/src/plays/dispatch.ts:172` (`deps.getAdapter(input.assignment.cli).build({ model: input.assignment.model, ... })`). Phase 1 shipped the PURE `resolveAssignmentModel({assignment, tiers})` + `detectModelCollapse`/`assertNoModelCollapse` in `packages/core/src/personas/resolve-model.ts`.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Add ONE shared async helper that bridges adapter I/O to the pure resolver — the SINGLE owner reused by this atom AND the next persona-level atom (do not inline resolve logic at the call site, and do not let the next atom need a second copy). RECOMMENDED: in `packages/core/src/personas/resolve-model.ts` add
     export async function resolveBuildModel(getAdapter: (cli: string) => { listModels(): Promise<{ tiers?: Readonly<Record<ModelTier, string>> }> }, assignment: { cli: string; model: string; tier?: ModelTier }): Promise<{ cli: string; model: string }>
   Behavior: if `assignment.model !== '' || assignment.tier === undefined`, return `resolveAssignmentModel({ assignment })` WITHOUT any adapter I/O (concrete pins and untiered assignments must NOT trigger a listModels() call). Only when a tier actually needs resolving, `await getAdapter(assignment.cli).listModels()` and pass its `tiers` into `resolveAssignmentModel`. Keep the parameter type minimal/structural so it does not force importing the full Adapter type if that creates a cycle; deviate only with a one-line justification. Export it from `packages/core/src/personas/index.ts` and the core index.
2. `packages/core/src/plays/dispatch.ts`: just before the `.build(...)` at line ~172, resolve the model via `resolveBuildModel(deps.getAdapter, input.assignment)` and pass the RESOLVED `model` into `.build({...})`. The resolver never changes `cli`, so the `getAdapter(input.assignment.cli)` call is unchanged. Everything else in dispatchPlay stays identical.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (add tests in the existing dispatch test file using the existing fake-getAdapter/runHeadless harness):
- A play assignment `{cli:'claude', model:'', tier:'strong'}` dispatched through `dispatchPlay` builds the command with the adapter's concrete strong model (fake adapter `listModels` returns `{ tiers: { default:'sonnet', strong:'opus' } }` → built `model` is `'opus'`). Assert on the model the fake adapter's `build` received.
- LIVE cross-CLI divergence (verified-when #5): the SAME `tier:'strong'` dispatched with two different fake adapters (one tiers strong->opus, the other strong->gpt-5-codex) builds with DIFFERENT concrete models — proving resolution hooks the live dispatch seam, not a dormant path.
- Concrete-pin unchanged (verified-when #2): `{cli, model:'sonnet'}` (no tier) builds with `'sonnet'` and the fake adapter's `listModels` is NEVER called (assert the spy/counter stays 0) — no behavior or extra I/O for the existing concrete-pin path.
- Untiered empty-model default: `{cli, model:''}` (no tier) builds with `''` and listModels is NOT called.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: all existing dispatch behavior (deterministic step, headless/visible branching, codex outPath handling); concrete pins byte-for-byte; no extra subprocess/listModels call on the concrete or untiered path. Run `pnpm -w typecheck` and `pnpm -w test`; paste exit results. NOTE: the daemon `mutations.test.ts` has a known pre-existing flake under parallel load (a different test fails per run) — if it appears, re-run that package once in isolation to confirm green and say so; it is not your regression. List files changed and the `resolveBuildModel` signature you settled on.

PHASE 2b of the model-tier priority: make PERSONA-level tiers take effect, and wire collapse detection — using the SAME `resolveBuildModel` helper from Phase 2 (do NOT add a second resolver). Resolve persona tier ONCE per launch path (single seam), not at each build site. Do NOT touch UI (that is Phase 3).

BACKGROUND (verified live): `ResolvedPersona` (`packages/core/src/personas/types.ts:60`) carries `{cli, model, mode}` but NOT tier. `resolveEffectivePersona` (`personas/effective.ts`) and `resolvePersona` (`personas/loader.ts`) build it. In `runRun` (`packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts:404`) the personas `oscar, bob, deb` arrive via `input` and `getAdapter` is in `deps`; the preflight loop at line ~447 (`getAdapter(p.cli).preflight(p.model)`) is the FIRST point all personas + getAdapter are together. Downstream build/driver sites (runner.ts:639 oscar spawn, :665 bob spawn; builder-driver.ts:84; oscar-driver.ts:76; observer.ts:55) all read `persona.model`. `oz-host.ts:160` builds the `oz` persona from `target.persona` (resolved at oz-host.ts:102). Phase 1 shipped `assertNoModelCollapse`/`detectModelCollapse`.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. `packages/core/src/personas/types.ts`: add `readonly tier?: ModelTier` to `ResolvedPersona`.
2. `resolveEffectivePersona` (effective.ts) AND `resolvePersona` (loader.ts): propagate `tier` from the assignment onto the returned `ResolvedPersona` using the same optional-spread idiom already used for `mode` (omit the key when tier is undefined). No other behavior change.
3. SINGLE-SEAM persona resolution in `runRun` (runner.ts): at the TOP of the run, BEFORE the preflight loop, resolve each of oscar/bob/(deb if present) via `await resolveBuildModel(getAdapter, persona)` to produce concrete-model ResolvedPersonas, and REBIND so every downstream site (preflight, spawn, headless drivers, any model display) uses the resolved persona. Net effect: a persona with `model:''` + `tier:'strong'` preflights and spawns on the adapter's concrete strong model; concrete pins and untiered personas are unchanged and trigger NO extra listModels() call (resolveBuildModel already short-circuits).
4. COLLAPSE DETECTION at that same seam: after resolving oscar and bob, call `assertNoModelCollapse(resolvedOscar, resolvedBob, ['oscar','bob'])` ONLY when the collapse is INTRODUCED BY TIER RESOLUTION — i.e. fire the check only if at least one of oscar/bob had `model===''` and a `tier` set (was tier-resolved). If BOTH are concrete `{cli,model}` pins, do NOT run the check (concrete pins are the founder's explicit choice and must behave exactly as today — verified-when #2). On collapse, fail the run through the run's normal fault path (a clear PreflightError-style reason naming both personas + the shared cli/model), before any spawn.
5. `oz-host.ts`: resolve the oz persona's tier via the same `resolveBuildModel(ctx.getAdapter, target.persona)` before the build at line ~160, passing the resolved model into `.build({...})`. Single persona, no collapse pair.

OUT OF SCOPE FOR THIS ATOM (do not touch; it is a separate small follow-up): the Oscar-Deb repair-dialogue persona path in `launcher.ts` (~1500/1779). Note it in your summary as the remaining persona seam so it is tracked, not hidden.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (tests):
- `resolveEffectivePersona` and `resolvePersona` propagate `tier` (present and absent cases).
- In the runner test harness: a tiered persona (model:'' , tier:'strong') causes preflight AND the spawned/built command to use the adapter's concrete strong model (fake adapter listModels returns tiers). A concrete-pinned persona is unchanged and listModels is NOT called for it.
- Collapse fires: oscar and bob both tier-resolving to the same {cli,model} fails the run before spawn with a clear two-persona reason. Collapse does NOT fire when both are concrete pins to the same model (today's behavior preserved).
- oz-host: a tiered `oz` persona builds its turn command on the resolved concrete model.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: every existing runner/oz-host/driver test; concrete-pin and untiered personas byte-for-byte with zero extra listModels() calls; preflight semantics. Run `pnpm -w typecheck` and `pnpm -w test`; paste exit results. The daemon `mutations.test.ts` has a known pre-existing flake under parallel load — if it appears, re-run that package in isolation to confirm and say so; it is not your regression. List files changed and where you placed the runner resolution + collapse check.

PHASE 3 of the model-tier priority: the Personas UI tier selector, end-to-end. Flow the Phase-0 tier metadata to the renderer and let the founder pick a TIER (not just a concrete model) per persona and per play, writing `tier` into assignments.json. ADAPTER-AGNOSTIC: derive tier options from the adapter's declared tiers — NO hardcoded cli ids or tier-name literals in the UI (verified-when #4). Do NOT change the resolver/runtime (Phases 1-2b own it); this atom only adds the UI + IPC plumbing + save.

PIPELINE (verified live): daemon `clis.ts` `cliView` builds a CliView whose `models` is the full `ModelListResult` (already carries `tiers` from Phase 0). The renderer IPC contract `packages/ui/src/main/ipc-contract.ts` has `CliModelsView = { canEnumerate, models, detail }` (NO tiers) inside `CliView`. `adaptCli` (`packages/ui/src/renderer/adapter.ts`, ~line 100) maps `view.models.*` into `Cli` (`packages/ui/src/renderer/model.ts`). `ModelControl` (`packages/ui/src/renderer/sections/Personas.tsx`, line ~18) renders the selector and calls `onChange(model: string)`; it is used for personas (line ~104) and sub-agents/plays (line ~173). IMPORTANT: the UI uses the sentinel string `'Default'` for the empty/CLI-default model (assignments.json stores `''`); a tier is only meaningful when the model is Default/empty.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. IPC contract (`ipc-contract.ts`): add `readonly tiers?: Readonly<Record<string, string>>` to `CliModelsView`.
2. Daemon serialization: ensure the CliView sent to the renderer carries `tiers` from `adapter.listModels()`. The daemon `cliView` already holds it via `ModelListResult`; if any explicit field-mapping to the IPC body exists (check `clis.ts`/`routes.ts`) that omits `tiers`, include it. Verify by tracing the value, do not assume.
3. `adaptCli` (`adapter.ts`): map `view.models.tiers` onto the renderer `Cli`. Add `readonly tiers?: Record<string, string>` to the `Cli` interface (`model.ts`).
4. Persona-edit model: add `tier?` to the renderer persona/sub-agent assignment types in `model.ts` that ModelControl edits (mirror wherever `model` lives), so the selector can carry it.
5. `ModelControl`: change `onChange` to carry both model and tier: `onChange(next: { model: string; tier?: string })`, and update both call sites (persona + sub-agent/play) to merge `next` into the edited object. When `cli.tiers` has entries, render a `Tiers` optgroup ABOVE the existing options whose entries are the tier-map KEYS (e.g. Default, Strong — capitalize for display, value carries the raw key). Selecting a tier => `onChange({ model: 'Default', tier: <key> })`. Selecting the existing Default / an enumerated concrete model / Custom => `onChange({ model: <value>, tier: undefined })` (concrete and tier are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE — choosing one clears the other). When the current assignment has a tier set, show that tier option as selected. Preserve ALL existing behavior (Default, enumerated select, Custom… free-text, the no-enumerate plain input) byte-for-byte when no tiers are declared or no tier is set.
6. Save path: wherever a persona/play assignment persists to assignments.json (trace from the persona-save IPC through `routes.ts`/`clis.ts`), write `tier` when set and OMIT it when unset; confirm a round-trip (set tier in UI -> assignments.json has tier -> reload shows the tier selected). Keep the `'Default'`<->`''` model conversion intact.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (UI vitest + any daemon route test needed):
- `adaptCli` maps a CliView with `models.tiers` to a `Cli` exposing those tiers; a CliView without tiers yields a Cli with no/empty tiers (no crash).
- `ModelControl` with a tier-declaring `cli` renders a Tiers group from the tier KEYS (assert the option labels come from the map, not hardcoded), and selecting a tier calls onChange with `{ model: 'Default', tier: <key> }`; selecting a concrete model calls onChange with `tier: undefined`.
- An assignment that already has `tier` shows the tier selected (not Custom/Default).
- Existing model selection (Default / enumerated / Custom / non-enumerate input) is unchanged when no tiers are present.
- Save round-trip persists `tier` to assignments.json and omits it when unset.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: all existing Personas UI behavior and tests; the `'Default'`<->`''` model mapping; CLIs that declare no tiers must render exactly as today. Run `pnpm -w typecheck` and `pnpm -w test` (the UI vitest + daemon suites). The daemon `mutations.test.ts` has a known pre-existing parallel-load flake — if it appears, re-run that package isolated to confirm and say so; not your regression. Paste exit results, list files changed, and describe the final ModelControl UX (option order + what each selection writes).

PHASE 4 (docs) of the model-tier priority: document the now-shipped model-tier feature in `docs/personas.md`. Documentation only — do NOT change any code, assignments.json, templates, or adapter behavior (tier DEFAULT adoption and the optional adapter hygiene are deliberately deferred to a founder decision; do not touch them). Write ONLY accurate, shipped behavior — do not claim defaults were changed or that any persona/play currently uses a tier.

ADD a new section to `docs/personas.md` (a good home is a `## Model assignment and tiers` section after `## Core personas` or near `## Write capability`; pick the cleanest placement). It must explain, accurately to what shipped in this priority:
- Each live persona has a `{cli, model}` in `cocoder/personas/assignments.json` (per persona, and per play via `plays`); `model: ""` means the CLI's default model.
- NEW: instead of hardcoding a concrete model id you can declare a `tier` (the canonical tiers today are `default` and `strong`) on a persona assignment OR a play assignment. The engine resolves the tier to a concrete `{cli, model}` at the live dispatch seam.
- PRECEDENCE: a non-empty concrete `model` ALWAYS wins; `tier` is consulted only when `model` is empty. Concrete pins behave exactly as before tiers existed.
- Each adapter declares its OWN tier->model mapping (in its `listModels` result), so the SAME tier resolves to DIFFERENT concrete models across CLIs (e.g. `strong` is opus on claude, gpt-5-codex on codex). Any adapter — including a future 4th one — participates automatically once it declares tier metadata; there is no per-adapter special-casing in the resolver or UI.
- COLLAPSE DETECTION: when a tier would resolve the adversarial Oscar/Bob pair to the same concrete model, the run fails fast before launch with a clear reason (this guard only fires when a tier introduced the collision; two explicit identical concrete pins are left as the founder's choice).
- UI: the Personas screen's Model control lists the CLI's declared tiers as selectable options; choosing a tier and choosing a concrete model are mutually exclusive.
- Briefly note that requesting a tier an adapter does not declare fails loudly with a clear error.
Update the file's `**Last verified:**` line to 2026-06-29 and mention this is the first-class model-tier capability. Keep the prose tight and consistent with the existing doc voice; do not duplicate content that the assignments.json schema or ADRs already own — link rather than restate where natural.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- `docs/personas.md` has an accurate model-assignment + tiers section covering: declaring tier per persona/play, concrete-pin-wins precedence, per-adapter tier maps + cross-CLI divergence, opt-in 4th-adapter participation, collapse detection, the UI selector, and fail-loud on undeclared tier.
- No code/config/template/adapter changes in the diff (docs only).
- Governance/doc-pinned checks stay green: run `pnpm -w test` (NOT just typecheck) so any doc-reference/drift/link checks that assert over `docs/**` pass; paste the exit results. The daemon `mutations.test.ts` has a known pre-existing parallel-load flake — if it appears, re-run that package isolated to confirm and say so; not your regression.

List the exact section you added and confirm the diff is docs-only.
